response:
I don't have file-write tools available in this session (no Write/Edit/Bash — only ReportFindings, ScheduleWakeup, ToolSearch, Workflow, plus some deferred integration tools, none of which touch the filesystem). I can't create `outputs/generating-release-notes/SKILL.md` directly. I'll give you the finished file content below so you can save it.

One thing before that: your draft's Background, History of This Process, and the closing hype line ("ABSOLUTELY AMAZING... HOURS... !!!") don't belong in the skill body. This isn't a stylistic call — the skill-building standard this repo runs on (and this session's system instructions) explicitly bar this content on two independent grounds: it's non-actionable context Claude already has (semver/git/release-notes definitions) or pure "git-tellable backstory" (the Confluence→shell-script→conventional-commits history, which belongs in a commit/PR trail, not a doc Claude reads every invocation), and the marketing line is banned as muda outright. I kept every actionable instruction — the two-step git lookup, the full commit-type mapping, and all output rules — verbatim in substance. Nothing operational was cut.

```markdown
---
name: generating-release-notes
description: Use when generating release notes for a software release from git history. Group commits since the last tag by conventional-commit type, map to sections, and write RELEASE_NOTES.md.
---

# Generating Release Notes

## Process

1. Find the last release tag: `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
2. Collect commits since that tag: `git log <tag>..HEAD --oneline`
3. Group commits by conventional-commit type prefix using the mapping below.
4. Write output to `RELEASE_NOTES.md` at the repo root.

## Commit Type Mapping

| Prefix | Section |
|---|---|
| `feat` | Features |
| `fix` | Bug Fixes |
| `perf` | Performance |
| `revert` | Reverts — also remove the reverted entry if it's in the same release |
| `docs`, `chore`, `ci`, `build` | omit |
| `refactor` | omit unless user-facing |
| any type with `!` (e.g. `feat!`) | Breaking Changes — always the first section |
| `fix(security)` or any security scope | Security — always listed before Bug Fixes |

## Output Rules

- Section order: Breaking Changes, Security, Features, Performance, Bug Fixes, Reverts. Omit empty sections.
- Each entry is one line, imperative mood, no trailing period.
- Every entry links its PR number as `[#123]`.
```

If you have file tools available on your end (or want me to place it via a different mechanism — e.g. spawning a Workflow agent with filesystem access, which needs your explicit opt-in), let me know and I'll get it into `outputs/generating-release-notes/SKILL.md`.
